channel.title: Tampa Bay News Monitor
channel.description: Press releases and breaking news, Tampa Bay

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Rare T. rex fossil sells for record $50.1 million at auction
item.description: A Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton from South Dakota has become the world’s most expensive fossil sold at auction, after fetching a record $50,130,000 at Sotheby’s in New York Tuesday.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Trump drops planned Strait of Hormuz toll in favor of Gulf trade deals
item.description: President Donald Trump reversed plans for a 20% Strait of Hormuz shipping fee, saying Gulf nations will instead pursue U.S. trade and investment deals.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Pinellas County Schools getting public input on next round of changes, potential school closures
item.description: The Pinellas County Schools is getting public input on next round of changes which could include potential school closures and consolidation to combat lower student enrollment and rising costs.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Olive Garden brings back Never Ending Pasta Pass after 6-year hiatus
item.description: Olive Garden is bringing back its Never Ending Pasta Pass for the first time in six years, offering 13 weeks of unlimited pasta for $100.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Atlanta tightens security for England-Argentina World Cup semifinal
item.description: Atlanta police are increasing security for Wednesday’s England-Argentina World Cup semifinal amid fan clashes and historic tensions.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Florida Rep. Buchanan speaks on daylight saving bill to be voted on in the House
item.description: Florida Congressman Vern Buchanan said his bill to make Daylight Saving Time permanent year-round will be voted on in the House of Representatives on Tuesday.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] NASA chief visits Russia's space launchpad for U.S.-Russian crew launch
item.description: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman attended a scheduled launch Tuesday of a U.S.-Russian crew to the International Space Station.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] We Are the Essentials assists PCSO in search for missing man from Frostproof
item.description: We Are the Essentials is assisting the Polk County Sheriff's Office in a search for a missing man from Frostproof.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Police arrest man for 988 crisis line swatting hoax at Bradenton marina
item.description: A Florida man is facing charges after police said they tied his devices to a swatting hoax at the Twin Dolphin Marina, according to the Bradenton Police Department.

item.title: [ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Jacked Panda Prep To Go
[ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Christmas in July arts and crafts show set for Venice Community Center on July 18
[ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Van Wezel kicks off annual ‘Christmas in July’ ticket sale
[ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] History Class Curriculum
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] Florida skywatchers can spot this rare comet during tonight's new moon
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] Couple gets violently sick from recalled Publix blueberries, files suit
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] Florida launches investigation into bread additive. Is your bread safe?
[City of Tampa] Tampa Police Partners with FDOT District Seven for Safer Roads
[ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Congo’s Ebola crisis deepens with most new cases unlinked to known contacts
item.description: WHO warns Congo’s Ebola outbreak is spreading faster than response efforts, with many cases tied to unknown transmission chains.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Trump administration opens endangered species’ habitats to development
item.description: The Trump administration finalized a rule Friday that changes how agencies enforce the Endangered Species Act and eliminates a key protection for imperiled wildlife against logging, oil drilling and other activities.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] An experimental Alzheimer's drug is showing some promise
item.description: An experimental drug might help slow early Alzheimer's disease in a markedly different way than today's treatments — by lowering levels of a brain protein called tau, researchers reported Tuesday.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Earthquake aid keeps flowing from Florida to Venezuela, as volunteers unite to help recovery
item.description: The 7.2- and 7.5-magnitude earthquakes struck 39 seconds apart on June 24, killing at least 4,500 people with thousands more still missing.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Inflation slowed in June as energy markets stabilized temporarily
item.description: Inflation eased in June as lower gas and energy prices helped cool consumer costs, though overall inflation remains above the Federal Reserve’s target.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool drained again amid ongoing repairs
item.description: Repairs continue on the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool after algae blooms, damage and green water plagued the $14 million renovation project.

item.title: [Tampa Bay Beacon (Pinellas)] St. Petersburg man killed in crash on Seminole Boulevard
[ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Man charged with instigating ‘swatting’ incident at Bradenton marina
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] Is Spotify down right now or is it just me? What to know
[The Florida Channel] 7/14/25 Social Services Estimating Conference: TANF Caseloads and Expenditures
item.description: 7/14/25 Social Services Estimating Conference: TANF Caseloads and Expenditures first appeared on The Florida Channel 7/14/25 Social Services Estimating Conference: TANF Caseloads and Expenditures first appeared on The Florida Channel

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Health officials eye lettuce in spreading Cyclospora outbreak
item.description: Health officials are investigating whether lettuce and salad greens are tied to a Cyclospora outbreak that has sickened more than 3,000 people in 31 states.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Susan Solves It: Hybrid sales surge
item.description: Tampa Bay 28 Reporter Susan El Khoury advises comparing long-term ownership costs, such as insurance, maintenance, and fuel savings, before choosing your next vehicle.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] News to Know for July 14
item.description: Florida prisoner set to be oldest inmate executed in state's modern history. Plus, Mega Millions jackpot grows to $637 million.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Florida prisoner, 74, set to be oldest inmate executed in state's modern history
item.description: Florida was again preparing Tuesday to execute one of its oldest death row prisoners in history, a 74-year-old convicted murderer who is one of three older inmates set to die in the span of a month.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Seminole crash kills 85-year-old man, passenger on life support: PCSO
item.description: An 85-year-old man was killed and his passenger remains on life support following a two-vehicle crash Monday evening in Seminole, according to the Pinellas County Sheriff's Office.

item.title: [Tampa Bay Beacon (Pinellas)] Forbici’s new Sundial location is now open. We got a first look.
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] Cases of cyclosporiasis in Florida? See cases by county in Florida
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] Is something development in the Gulf? Here's what you should know

[ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Florida voters want answers on rising insurance costs; here's where the candidates stand
item.description: Florida voters frustrated by skyrocketing homeowners' insurance premiums want to make sure state leaders are listening.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Mega Millions jackpot grows to $637 million
item.description: Mega Millions jackpot climbs to $637 million after no winner is announced in Friday's drawing. The next drawing will be Tuesday at 11 p.m.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] 1 dead, 1 on life support after deadly Seminole crash: Pinellas deputies
item.description: The Pinellas County Sheriff's Office (PCSO) said they are investigating a fatal crash that left one dead and one on life support in Seminole on Monday night.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Southbound lanes of U.S. 19 in Tarpon Springs shut down due to crash: TSPD
item.description: The southbound lanes of U.S. Highway 19 in Tarpons Springs are closed due to a crash early Tuesday morning, authorities said.
